ranges.reason: Introduction to succession myth as propaganda; contrast with biblical failed rebellion
Explanation of rebellion as internal dethronement of God in hearts
Devil's attack on creatures out of hatred for God; link to Genesis 3
Wordplay on 'nachash' (serpent/shining one/deceiver) in Genesis 3
Seraphim as serpentine angels; cherubim as guardians; connection to Ezekiel 28
Curse on serpent in Genesis 3:14-15; devil becomes eater of dead, lord of death
Isaiah and Ezekiel retell the story correcting pagan myths
Reading of Isaiah 14:3-21 (Lucifer passage)
Explanation of Isaiah 14: Lucifer from 1 Enoch; Helel ben Shachar as Enlil; correction of succession myth
Ezekiel 28: prophecy against king of Tyre; connection to Baal cycle and failed insurrection
Recap: five rebellions; different types of angels become different evil spirits
No hierarchy among demons; they are spirits of chaos and destruction
Caller Bob: question on devil's motivation; answer: envy of humanity, war of attrition
Devil's rebellion is propaganda; he attacks humanity; baptismal renunciation as leaving rebellion
Transition to second fall of angels
Second fall: Watchers giving forbidden knowledge; pagan narratives (Sumerian King List, Apkallu, Prometheus); corrected in Genesis 4-6
Genesis 6:1-4: sons of God and daughters of men; Nephilim
Nephilim as giants; Og's bed (Deuteronomy 3:11); connection to Babylonian ritual bed; two-thirds god, one-third human origin
Third fall: origin of unclean spirits from dead Nephilim; book of Jubilees; demons afflict wicked for repentance
Demonic affliction as opportunity for repentance; connection to coronavirus; St. Paul on delivering to Satan
Fourth fall: Tower of Babel; pagan narrative (Plato, 70 sons of El); corrected in Genesis 11; Deuteronomy 32:8-9; sons of God allotted to nations
God's withdrawal as mercy; sons of God accept worship (Deuteronomy 32:17); territorial spirits; St. Paul on demons
Caller Mihai: question on demons' direct physical interaction; examples from Job, Tobit, Revelation
Fifth fall: Satan cast down in Christ's ministry; Luke 10:17-20; Revelation 12; distinction between devil and Satan; St. Andrew of Caesarea; unity of material and spiritual worlds
Conclusion: preview of next episode on sons of God and theosis; final comment on unity of creation
Pre-show banter, technical issues, and introductory pleasantries
Fragmentary line
Tangent on Western focus on Adam and Eve; mention of future episode
Restatement of devil as lord of death; hemnography of Pascha
Break announcement, station ID, and sponsor ad
Caller introduction pleasantries
Caller closing pleasantries
Teasing future giants episode; banter about listener complaints
Transition banter
Second break, sponsor ad, and upcoming episode promotion
Caller introduction and personal pleasantries
Caller closing
Fragmentary line
Outro, closing announcements, and scripture reading
